_contract: flag.json — the ONLY interface between intake.py (writer) and the OBS overlay (reader).
_rules: intake.py writes this file; the overlay polls it. Neither imports the other.
This is the shape a V2 mid-stream overlay pop would consume. Build once, both consume it (page spec sec 10).
NEVER a dollar value. NEVER a predicted grade. NEVER a score. The stream VOD becomes the shop provenance link; anything here travels to the buyer.
Every price-like fact is COST (from fees.json, verified+dated), never VALUE (needs comps, human, off-stream).

_states.scanning: sensor is mid-pass; overlay reveals the raster as it arrives
_states.look: flag fired — this card is worth a closer look
_states.pass: matched neither watchlist nor structural rule; logged, not discarded
_states.unresolved: identity known, rarity NOT resolvable from this capture — the honest refusal

card.name: Meowth ex
card._image_note: written progressively during scanning if the driver supports strip output; else written once on completion

flag._reason_note: Render reasons verbatim. They are the interest signal. Do not summarize into a number.

centering._note: null until a flatbed pass exists. Phone/webcam capture flags but does not measure (SPEC_card_intake sec 9). When present, render as the payoff line: 'CENTERING  L-R 52/48'.

grading_cost._note: COST only, straight from fees.json. Never implies the card is WORTH grading — that verdict needs comps and is off-stream.
grading_cost.text: Grading (PSA Regular): $122.47 all-in · card gone ~85 days
